title: Tigridia convoluta
intro: Tigridia convoluta, also known as the Mexican daisy or tiger flower, is a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Tigridia convoluta include iridoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of ailments, from skin conditions to respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Tigridia convoluta is a species of flowering plant in the iris family, Iridaceae. This plant is native to Mexico and is also known as the Mexican shell flower. Tigridia convoluta is a perennial plant with a unique, folded or "convoluted" flower that comes in a variety of colors. The plant has strap-like leaves and produces funnel-shaped flowers in shades of pink, yellow, white, and red. Tigridia convoluta is often grown as an ornamental plant in warm climates for its vibrant, exotic blooms.
